element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Opponent has slow guard and reaction time; up to 3 consecutive body blows can land before he reacts
Foundation technique: exploit opponent's slow guard by tapping up to raise guard, then immediately body blow before they react ('gutters')
Counter-punch opponent directly before/after their attacks; max 3 stars storable; lost on hit or knockdown
Only executable with earned stars; different opponents require different timing windows
Basic defensive maneuver; slower than duck (1 frame slower in TAS context)
Faster defensive option than dodge; 1 frame advantage in execution; riskier in real-time play due to precision requirement
Raises guard to block; depletes heart counter when blocking opponent punches

Heart counter decreases on: being hit, blocking punch, throwing missed/blocked punch. At zero, Mac turns pink and exhausted—cannot attack but can still dodge/duck/block. Regain hearts only by avoiding opponent attacks
Each opponent follows set pattern requiring trial-and-error learning and memorization; same opponent in later circuits uses new attacks forcing new strategy

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Don Flamenco (Minor Circuit, Bout 2) or earlier
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Opponent or Mac can be knocked down; 10-second count to get up; max 3 knockdowns per bout without TKO loss; 4th knockdown = automatic loss
3 knockdowns in one round = automatic TKO loss for player
Bouts can last full 3 rounds; decision win possible by point accumulation if opponent not KO'd (some bouts auto-lose if not KO'd)
Points awarded for landing punches; certain bouts require KO/TKO to win and cannot be won by decision
Loss to ranked opponent (non-title) grants rematch chance; loss to title opponent causes rank fall (1 place Minor/Major, 2 places World)
Three circuits: Minor (3 opponents), Major (4 opponents), World (6 opponents), plus Dream Fight finale. Repeated losses force rank fallback

element_introduction_order.element: Piston Honda (repeat opponent, Major→World with new attacks)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Returns in World Circuit with new attack patterns; charge mechanic exploitable for clock-stop glitch in TAS; requires new strategy vs. initial encounter

element_introduction_order.element: Bald Bull (repeat opponent with signature attack 'Bull Charge')
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Bull Charge is timed attack; successful counter instantly knocks opponent down; no entrance music (differs from other fighters)

element_introduction_order.element: Don Flamenco (repeat opponent, Minor→World with new attacks)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Reappears in World Circuit with new pattern; vulnerable to jabs and body blows; exploitable for clock-stop glitch variations

element_introduction_order.element: Great Tiger (World Circuit signature mechanic: teleport/dodge pattern)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Signature pattern-based fighter; no entrance music; pattern-dependent victory requirement

element_introduction_order.element: King Hippo (unique sprite design, vulnerable mouth)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Only character with unique sprite (not reused); weakness: open mouth window; harder pattern to exploit than earlier opponents

element_introduction_order.element: Super Macho Man / Mr. Dream (final boss, Complex pattern)
element_introduction_order.first_appears: World Circuit Title Bout (Gold version) or Dream Fight (Tyson version)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Final boss in standard play; significantly harder pattern; dream bout for Tyson version requires password or full circuit completion

element_introduction_order.element: Mike Tyson (final Dream Fight boss, Tyson version only)
element_introduction_order.first_appears: Dream Fight (Tyson version only; password or completion-gated)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Most difficult opponent; only accessible via: password (007 373 5963), Another World Circuit mode (password 135 792 4680), or perfect circuit completion

typical_relief_triggers: Opponent knockdown (10-second count reset provides breathing room)
Mac regaining hearts via successful dodges during exhaustion state
Transitioning between rounds (reset attack patterns, fresh round clock)
typical_tension_triggers: Heart counter depletion (Mac enters pink exhausted state, cannot attack)
Mac getting hit (immediate heart loss and potential knockdown risk)
Opponent pattern shifts mid-bout or between circuit encounters
Timed special attacks (e.g., Bald Bull's Bull Charge) requiring precise counter
Bout structure: must KO opponent (decision auto-loss in certain fights)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical tension-relief loop: player must memorize and execute opponent patterns under heart-depletion pressure. Rhythm is pattern-interrupt based rather than time-pressure based. Early Minor Circuit opponents have slow guard/reaction allowing safe experimental play; progression introduces faster pattern cycles and no-decision-win restrictions, forcing knockdown-based victories. Knockdown mechanic provides natural narrative beats. Three-round structure with intra-round pattern consistency creates predictable rhythm windows

spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden passwords reveal secret circuits and bosses (Another World Circuit with Tyson accessible via 135 792 4680 + A+B+Select; direct Tyson Dream Fight via 007 373 5963; credits view via 106 113 0120 + A+B+Select). Limited Edition gold cartridge contained inaccessible Tyson/Another World data in ROM but no callable routines until later versions

difficulty_curve.early_game: Glass Joe and Don Flamenco (Minor Circuit) establish fundamentals with slow guard reaction, allowing safe multi-hit combos and error tolerance. Introduction to core mechanics: jabs, body blows, stars, uppercuts, defense. Low punishment for failed patterns
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Major Circuit (Piston Honda, Bald Bull, Don Flamenco II, King Hippo) escalates pattern complexity and introduces timed signature attacks (Bull Charge). Heart depletion becomes more punishing; fewer safe opening windows. Requires pattern memorization and precision timing
difficulty_curve.late_game: World Circuit (6 opponents including repeat fighters with new patterns, Great Tiger, King Hippo, Super Macho Man) removes decision-win option for most bouts, forcing knockouts. Patterns significantly accelerated. Mike Tyson/Mr. Dream Dream Fight is peak difficulty—complex pattern, high knockdown risk, minimal safe windows
notable_spikes: Bald Bull Major Circuit introduction (Bull Charge signature attack requires specific counter timing)
Great Tiger World Circuit (teleport/dodge pattern unpredictability vs. prior pattern-lock design)
King Hippo World Circuit (unique vulnerability window timing)
Mike Tyson Dream Fight (highest complexity pattern; fastest attack cycles)

notable_level_patterns.description: Foundational strategy applicable to slow-guard opponents (Glass Joe, Don Flamenco, Soda Popinski early encounters). Hold Up to raise opponent guard, release and immediately body blow before guard reacts. Up to 3 consecutive gutters possible on minor circuit opponents before they adapt. Creates safe opening for accumulating knockdowns without taking damage
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Glass Joe (Minor Circuit Bout 1), Don Flamenco (Minor and World Circuits)

notable_level_patterns.description: Opponent throws specific attack (hook, uppercut, jab); player counters with own punch in precise window before/after opponent attack, earning star. Stars required for powered uppercut. Up to 3 stars storable; lost on hit or knockdown. Different opponents have different counter windows and attack triggers

Opponent executes predictable, timed signature move (e.g., Bald Bull charges straight at Mac). Requires player counter-punch within narrow timing window. Successful counter results in instant knockdown. Failure results in heavy damage. Creates high-tension, high-reward moment
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Bald Bull (Major Circuit and World Circuit)

notable_level_patterns.description: Opponent reappears in later circuit with new attack set, forcing player to develop new strategy despite prior familiarity. Piston Honda appears Major→World; Don Flamenco appears Minor→World. Establishes progression through familiarity-breaking mechanic
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Piston Honda II (World Circuit), Don Flamenco II (World Circuit)

notable_level_patterns.description: Certain bouts (especially World Circuit onwards, and Dream Fight) cannot be won by decision/point accumulation. Player must achieve KO or TKO to win; bout auto-fails if 3 rounds complete without knockdown. Increases tension and forces offensive playstyle
notable_level_patterns.example_level: World Circuit Title Bouts; Dream Fight

notable_level_patterns.description: When heart counter reaches zero, Mac turns pink, becomes exhausted, cannot attack, but can still dodge/duck/block. Regain hearts only by successfully avoiding opponent attacks. Creates rhythm break: shift from offensive memorization to pure defensive positioning. Resets on new round or knockdown
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All opponents; occurs during extended negative exchanges

notable_level_patterns.description: Three knockdowns in single round trigger TKO loss. Each knockdown = 10-second count to recover. Creates escalating tension narrative: first knockdown is recovery opportunity, third approaches end-of-bout threat. Fourth knockdown = auto-loss regardless of round
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All opponents; particularly dangerous in World Circuit where no-decision rules apply
